title: buildah bud walks symlinks
body:
After a symlink is created, buildah bud would fail if the symlink was
mounted as a volume.

Fixes https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/439

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Doing the symlink calls inside of a chroot.
Cannot use `filepath.EvalSymlinks()` as there may be paths with non-existent directories/files and `filepath.EvalSymlinks()` calls `lstat` internally, which throws a directory does not exist error. So doing it with a loop and `Lstat` and `ReadLink`.
I put the chroot code under `imagebuildah` in the file `chroot_symlink.go`, not sure where exactly to put. Can move it around based on where it fits better.
Added tests for the various cases I could think of.
@rhatdan @nalind PTAL
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 5ce8009) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

:pushpin: Commit 4cac403 has been approved by `ripcurld`
